steps.title: Use the factor theorem
steps.content: If $x + 24y$ is a factor of an expression, then setting $x = -24y$ must make the expression equal zero (factor theorem).

steps.title: Substitute into each choice
steps.content: Substitute $x = -24y$ into each answer choice and check for zero.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $x^{2}+25xy+24y^{2}$: Substitute $x=-24y$: $576y^2 - 600y^2 + 24y^2 = 0$, so $x+24y$ is a factor. ✓
  • Choice B — $x^{2}+25xy+25y^{2}$: Substitute $x=-24y$: $576y^2 - 600y^2 + 25y^2 = y^2$, not zero. ✗
  • Choice C — $x^{2}+72xy+72y^{2}$: Substitute $x=-24y$: $576y^2 - 1728y^2 + 72y^2 = -1080y^2$, not zero. ✗
  • Choice D — $x^{2}+50xy+75y^{2}$: Substitute $x=-24y$: $576y^2 - 1200y^2 + 75y^2 = -549y^2$, not zero. ✗
That confirms A is correct.

eliminationHtml:
• Choice A — $x^{2}+25xy+24y^{2}$: Substitute $x=-24y$: $576y^2 - 600y^2 + 24y^2 = 0$. Zero confirms $x+24y$ is a factor. ✓
  • Choice B — $x^{2}+25xy+25y^{2}$: Substitute $x=-24y$: $576y^2 - 600y^2 + 25y^2 = y^2 eq 0$. ✗
  • Choice C — $x^{2}+72xy+72y^{2}$: Substitute $x=-24y$: $576y^2 - 1728y^2 + 72y^2 = -1080y^2 eq 0$. ✗
  • Choice D — $x^{2}+50xy+75y^{2}$: Substitute $x=-24y$: $576y^2 - 1200y^2 + 75y^2 = -549y^2 eq 0$. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
